document.source_title: PCOS, the most common cause of infertility, to now be called PMOS

claims.claim_text: PCOS is being renamed to PMOS.
claims.evidence_text: A disorder that affects 170 million women worldwide and is the leading cause of infertility, known as polycystic ovary syndrome, or PCOS, is being renamed to improve diagnosis and care.

entities.name: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome
entities.description: A disorder affecting women's hormonal and metabolic health, leading to infertility.
entities.evidence_text: A disorder that affects 170 million women worldwide and is the leading cause of infertility, known as polycystic ovary syndrome, or PCOS.

entities.name: Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome
entities.description: The new name for PCOS, chosen to better reflect the condition's impacts.
entities.evidence_text: The new name, polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome, or PMOS, was chosen by a global coalition of patients, clinicians and medical organisations.

entities.name: Androgen Excess and PCOS Society
entities.description: An organization involved in the consensus to rename PCOS.
entities.evidence_text: The consensus to rename the condition, led by several research groups and the Androgen Excess and PCOS Society.

events.event: Announcement of the renaming of PCOS to PMOS
events.evidence_text: Researchers announced on Tuesday (May 12, 2026) at a medical meeting.

events.event: Publication of research letter in JAMA Internal Medicine
events.evidence_text: Dr. Terhi Piltonen of the University of Oulu in Finland wrote in a research letter published on Monday (May 11, 2026) in JAMA Internal Medicine.

metrics.name: Number of women affected by PCOS
metrics.evidence_text: A disorder that affects 170 million women worldwide.

metrics.name: Survey responses for renaming consensus
metrics.evidence_text: The consensus to rename the condition was based on more than 14,000 survey responses from patients and health professionals worldwide.

timelines.milestone: Renaming of PCOS to PMOS announced
timelines.evidence_text: Researchers announced on Tuesday (May 12, 2026) at a medical meeting.

timelines.milestone: Research letter published in JAMA Internal Medicine
timelines.evidence_text: Dr. Terhi Piltonen of the University of Oulu in Finland wrote in a research letter published on Monday (May 11, 2026) in JAMA Internal Medicine.

citations.title: PCOS, the most common cause of infertility, to now be called PMOS

source_credibility.reason: The information is based on announcements from researchers and publications in reputable medical journals.
